title: First-run UX: make GOAL a mandatory /evo:loop input — the handler MUST ask the user to input a goal before dispatching (never run a goal-less loop → rc=10). Fix SKILL.md docs + handler-prompt rule + regression test.
root_cause: The loop binary requires a goal (go/cmd/evolve/cmd_loop_args.go:151-156 — goal required unless --resume/--dry-run; goal-directed design). But skills/loop/SKILL.md presents the goal as OPTIONAL: the frontmatter argument-hint (line 4) and Usage (line 160) render it as `[goal]`, and Quick Start (line 44) says only that the CYCLE COUNT is optional. STRICT MODE then tells the slash-command handler to dispatch `evolve loop <args>` verbatim, so a first-time user who runs bare `/evo:loop` (no goal) dispatches a goal-less command → exit rc=10 → the first experience is a raw 'a goal is required' error rather than a guided prompt. Reproduced live 2026-07-18: `evolve loop --strategy balanced` with no goal → rc=10.
evidence: go/cmd/evolve/cmd_loop_args.go:151-156 (goal-required gate); skills/loop/SKILL.md:4 (argument-hint marks goal optional '[goal]'), :44 (only cycle-count-optional stated), :160 (Usage marks goal optional); go/cmd/evolve/dispatch_test.go:99 (binary's 'a goal is required' contract already asserted — the doc diverged from it). VERIFIED NON-ISSUE (no action): the SELF_SHA boot-halt is not a fresh-install condition — .evolve/state.json is gitignored (.gitignore:35) and absent from .goreleaser.yml archives, so a fresh install has no pin and cmd_loop_boot_recovery.go:141-153 short-circuits → boots clean.
fix: Doc + handler-behavior only; DO NOT change the binary's goal-required contract (it is intentional and correct). SCOPE per operator: make the goal MANDATORY and ASK the user for it before acting — nothing more (no discovery/no-goal mode). (1) skills/loop/SKILL.md argument-hint (line 4) + Usage (line 160): mark the goal REQUIRED, e.g. `(--resume | [--cycles N] [strategy] <goal>)` with `<goal>` not `[goal]`. (2) Quick Start (lines 44 + 160-175): state plainly 'A GOAL is REQUIRED (except --resume/--dry-run); only the cycle COUNT is optional.' (3) STRICT MODE handler rule (authoritative): 'Before dispatching, if $ARGUMENTS contains no goal and it is not --resume/--dry-run, you MUST ask the user to input a one-line goal (AskUserQuestion or a direct prompt) and WAIT for their answer BEFORE running `evolve loop`. Never dispatch a goal-less loop.' (4) rc-table: map rc=10 to 're-prompt the user for a goal', not a terminal error.
acceptance: Regression test (Go, go/cmd/evolve or a skills-doc-lint test): reads skills/loop/SKILL.md and asserts the argument-hint AND Usage mark the goal REQUIRED (no optional `[goal]` bracket); fails if a future edit re-introduces optional-goal framing — locks doc/binary agreement
SKILL.md STRICT MODE contains an explicit, imperative rule that the handler must PROMPT the user for a goal (and wait) when none is supplied, and must NOT dispatch a goal-less `evolve loop`
SKILL.md rc-table maps rc=10 to a user re-prompt for the goal
cd go && go test ./cmd/evolve/... -race — N/N PASS, no regression; apicover clean
source: operator-injection 2026-07-18: user explicitly directed 'make goal a mandatory requirement, asking the user to input it before act'. Reproduced the rc=10 first-run failure and root-caused the SKILL.md/binary divergence.
